title: The Glass Lantern
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In 1925 Istanbul, a master glassblower fights to clear his name when his lantern is found at a murdered diplomat's scene.
long_description:
The Glass Lantern follows Kemal Özden, a master glassblower in 1925 Istanbul whose distinctive workshop lantern is discovered shattered beside the body of a British consular secretary. Inspector Leyla Aksoy — one of the first women in the Turkish Republic's police force — draws the case, and her investigation peels back layers of post-Ottoman intrigue, antiquities smuggling, and a forgery ring stretching from the Grand Bazaar to Venice and London.

As Kemal navigates suspicion, family secrets, and the legacy of his estranged uncle — a legendary glassmaker on Murano — the novel becomes a meditation on craft, memory, and the truths that fragile things can hold. The glass lantern serves as a central metaphor: luminous, breakable, and capable of casting different truths depending on the light.
